description_long: This object is a roughly cuboidal stone block with rounded edges and corners, giving it a weathered and aged appearance. Its surface is mottled with a mix of pale lavender and yellowish-green hues, suggesting mineral deposits or lichen growth. The block features several shallow, irregular depressions and pockmarks distributed across its faces, adding to its rugged texture. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible seams or artificial markings. Its finish is matte and slightly rough, indicating it has been exposed to the elements for some time. The overall form and coloration suggest it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: A weathered, rounded cuboidal stone block with mottled lavender and yellow-green coloration and pockmarked, rough surfaces.
description_view_0: This view shows three faces with prominent pockmarks and a rounded top, highlighting the mottled coloration and surface irregularities.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the smoother, less pockmarked face, with subtle color variations and a more continuous surface.
description_view_2: This side displays several clustered depressions and a slightly more angular edge, with visible color speckling.
description_view_3: This view reveals a face with multiple shallow indentations and a generally rough, uneven surface texture.
attribute_installation_method: set in sand or directly on compacted soil
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows mottled discoloration and shallow pockmarks, suggesting exposure to weather and possible lichen or mineral buildup.
